_meta.source: Fabricated reasonable real-food content. Web access to realfoodwin.org was unavailable during this seeding pass (WebFetch/WebSearch/curl all blocked by permission policy). All entries below are marked fabricated:true and reflect plausible content for the Real Food Win catalog given the project spec (junk-food swap targets like Snickers, Doritos, Big Mac, Oreos; brands directory for real-food alternatives). Replace with scraped content from the live site when access is granted.
_meta.intended_use: Greenfield rebuild seed data for recipes + brands tables (see realfoodwinprojectspec.md section 5.1)

recipes.title: Homemade Snickers Bites
ingredients: 1 cup pitted Medjool dates (about 12), soaked in warm water 10 minutes then drained
1/2 cup natural peanut butter (just peanuts + salt)
1 cup dark chocolate chips (70% or higher)
steps: Line an 8x8 baking dish with parchment paper, leaving overhang on two sides.
Process the drained dates in a food processor until they form a thick, smooth paste, scraping down the sides as needed.
Add the peanut butter, vanilla, and fine sea salt to the date paste. Pulse until fully combined and the mixture pulls away from the sides.
Fold the chopped peanuts into the date-peanut mixture with a spatula.
Press the mixture firmly and evenly into the lined dish. Freeze for 20 minutes until firm.
Melt the chocolate chips and coconut oil together in 30-second microwave bursts, stirring between each, until smooth.
Lift the firmed slab out of the dish and cut into 16 squares.
Dip each square in the melted chocolate, letting excess drip off, and place on parchment.
Sprinkle each bite with flaky sea salt while the chocolate is still wet.
Refrigerate 15 minutes until the chocolate sets. Store in the fridge in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks.

recipes.title: Crispy Baked Real-Cheese Tortilla Chips
ingredients: 10 small corn tortillas (look for ingredients: corn, lime, water, salt)
1/2 cup finely grated aged cheddar (real cheese, not powder)
steps: Heat the oven to 400F (205C). Line two sheet pans with parchment.
Stack the tortillas and cut each into 6 wedges.
Toss the wedges in a large bowl with the avocado oil until evenly coated.
In a small bowl, whisk together the salt, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, and cayenne.
Sprinkle the spice blend over the oiled tortillas and toss again to coat.
Spread the chips in a single layer across the two sheet pans, not overlapping.
Bake for 8 minutes, then rotate the pans and bake 5-7 more minutes until deeply golden and crisp.
While the chips are hot, toss in a clean bowl with the grated cheddar and nutritional yeast so they melt and cling.
Cool 5 minutes before serving so the cheese sets onto the chips.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container at room temp for up to 4 days.

recipes.title: Grass-Fed Smash Burgers with House Sauce
ingredients: 4 slices sharp cheddar or American (from a real-food brand)
4 brioche or sourdough buns, split and toasted
1 cup shredded iceberg or romaine lettuce
1 tbsp grass-fed butter or beef tallow for the pan
steps: Make the house sauce: whisk the mayo, ketchup, mustard, pickle brine, and smoked paprika in a small bowl. Refrigerate while you cook.
Divide the beef into 4 equal balls without overworking. Season the outside of each lightly with salt and pepper.
Heat a cast-iron skillet or griddle over high heat until smoking. Add the butter or tallow.
Place a beef ball in the pan and immediately smash flat with a heavy spatula or burger press until 1/4 inch thick. Repeat in batches; do not crowd the pan.
Cook 90 seconds without moving so a deep crust forms. Flip, top each patty with a slice of cheese, and cook 60 more seconds.
Toast the buns in the pan drippings for 30 seconds, cut-side down.
Spread house sauce on both halves of each bun. Build: bottom bun, lettuce, pickles, patty, thin onion, top bun.
Serve immediately while the patties are crackling hot.

recipes.title: Almond Flour Chocolate Sandwich Cookies
ingredients: 1/2 cup powdered sugar (or coconut sugar blended fine)
3 tbsp grass-fed butter or palm shortening, softened
1 tbsp full-fat coconut milk or heavy cream
steps: Heat the oven to 350F (175C). Line a sheet pan with parchment.
In a bowl, whisk the almond flour, cocoa, salt, and baking soda.
In a second bowl, whisk the maple syrup, melted coconut oil, and vanilla until smooth.
Pour the wet into the dry and stir until a stiff dough forms. If sticky, chill 10 minutes.
Scoop teaspoons of dough and roll into 24 small balls. Flatten each to 1/4 inch on the sheet pan using the bottom of a glass.
Bake 8-10 minutes until set but still soft. Cool fully on the pan (they crisp as they cool).
Make the filling: beat the powdered sugar, butter, coconut milk, vanilla, and pinch of salt until fluffy and pipeable.
Match cookies into pairs by size. Pipe or spread a teaspoon of filling onto the flat side of one cookie and sandwich with its match.
Press gently. Let sit 10 minutes for the filling to firm up before serving.
Store in an airtight container at room temp 3 days, or refrigerate up to a week.

recipes.title: Five-Minute Overnight Oats with Real Berries
ingredients: 1/2 cup whole milk, oat milk, or almond milk
1 tbsp pure maple syrup or raw honey
1 tsp bee pollen or hemp hearts (optional)
steps: In a half-pint jar or sealable container, add the oats, milk, yogurt, chia, maple syrup, vanilla, cinnamon, and salt.
Stir thoroughly so the chia seeds disperse and don't clump at the bottom.
Seal and refrigerate at least 6 hours, ideally overnight.
In the morning, give it a stir. If too thick, splash in a tablespoon more milk.
Top with the berries and nuts. Add bee pollen or hemp hearts if using.
Eat cold from the jar, or warm gently in a saucepan for a hot breakfast.

recipes.title: Real-Ingredient Chicken Tenders with Honey Mustard
ingredients: 1 1/4 lb boneless skinless chicken tenders or breast strips
1 1/2 cups panko breadcrumbs (or crushed pork rinds for grain-free)
steps: Soak the chicken in the milk + 1 tsp Dijon mixture for 15 minutes (or up to overnight) while you set up the breading station.
Heat the oven to 425F (220C). Place a wire rack on a sheet pan and lightly oil it.
Stir together the panko, parmesan, garlic powder, paprika, onion powder, salt, and pepper in a wide bowl.
Set up three stations: flour in one bowl, beaten eggs in a second, the seasoned panko in a third.
Lift each tender from the milk soak, dredge in flour, dip in egg, then press into the panko mixture so it sticks all over.
Place on the wire rack. Repeat with all tenders.
Drizzle the tops with avocado oil. Bake 18-22 minutes, flipping halfway, until deeply golden and cooked to 165F internal.
While they bake, whisk the honey, 3 tbsp Dijon, and apple cider vinegar in a small bowl for the dipping sauce.
Let the tenders rest 3 minutes, then serve hot with the honey mustard.

brands.name: Simple Mills
brands.description: Almond-flour based crackers, cookies, and baking mixes with short, recognizable ingredient lists. A reliable swap for grocery-aisle ultra-processed snacks when home baking isn't an option.

brands.name: Applegate
brands.category: Deli meats, hot dogs, breakfast sausage
brands.description: Humanely raised meats with no antibiotics, no added nitrates or nitrites, and no artificial ingredients. Use them as 1:1 replacements for conventional deli and breakfast meats.

brands.name: Kettle & Fire
brands.description: Slow-simmered bone broths made from grass-fed beef and organic chicken bones with vegetables and herbs. Shelf-stable cartons make it easy to keep real broth on hand for cooking and sipping.

brands.name: Siete Foods
brands.description: Grain-free tortillas and chips made from cassava, almond flour, chickpea flour, and avocado oil. Family-founded, designed for households navigating gluten and grain sensitivities.

brands.name: Maple Hill Creamery
brands.description: 100% grass-fed organic dairy from small family farms in upstate New York. Higher omega-3 content than conventional dairy and produced without grain feed.

brands.name: Primal Kitchen
brands.description: Avocado-oil based mayo, ketchup, dressings, and BBQ sauces with no canola, soybean, or sugar fillers. The fastest way to replace seed-oil and HFCS-heavy condiments in a pantry overhaul.
